question:
Suppose the life of a certain security alarm system is exponential
distribution with constant failure rate λ = 0.00008 failures per hour.
(a) What is the probability that a security system will last at least 10,000 h?
(b) What is the probability that a security system will last at least 10,000 h given
that it has already survived 5,000 h?
answer:
(a) The probability that a security system will last at least $10,000 \mathrm{~h}$ is

$$
P\{T \geq 10,000\}=e^{-\lambda t} e^{-(0.00008)(10,000)}=e^{-0.8}=0.4493
$$

(b) The probability that a security system will last at least $10,000 \mathrm{~h}$ given that it has already survived $5,000 \mathrm{~h}$ is

$$
\begin{aligned}
P\{T \geq 10,000 \mid T \geq 5,000\} & =P\{T \geq 5,000\} \\
& =e^{-\lambda t}=e^{-(0.00008)(5,000)}=0.6703
\end{aligned}
$$
